task_completion.description: Link the 'RoboArm_Collection' from the master rig file into the current scene.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Outliner, 'RoboArm_Collection' is visible, typically with a chain-link icon indicating it is linked from an external file. The RoboArm armature should appear in the 3D Viewport alongside the yellow sphere.

task_completion.description: Create a library override on the linked collection to make the armature poseable.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Outliner, the chain-link icon next to 'RoboArm_Collection' or its contents changes to a library override icon (a chain link with a small arrow/character). The armature becomes selectable and switchable to Pose Mode.

task_completion.description: Pose the arm so it reaches towards the yellow sphere.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 3D Viewport (in Pose Mode), the bones of the RoboArm (specifically the UpperArm and Forearm) are rotated away from their default straight position and are visually pointing or reaching towards the yellow target sphere.

task_completion.description: Insert a rotation keyframe for the bones at Frame 1.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Timeline or Dope Sheet editor shows the playhead at Frame 1, with yellow diamond icons indicating keyframes have been inserted for the armature's bone rotations. The bone transformation properties (rotation) in the sidebar (N-panel) or properties editor may also be highlighted in yellow.

task_completion.description: Save the resulting file to the specified output path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Blender application window title bar at the top displays the new filename 'shot_01_animated.blend' or the full path '/home/ga/BlenderProjects/shot_01_animated.blend', indicating the file has been successfully saved.

integrity.description: The agent used the Blender GUI to perform the tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent navigating Blender menus (e.g., File > Link, Object > Library Override), using the 3D viewport gizmos, and interacting with the Timeline/Outliner, rather than just running a Python script in the terminal.

integrity.description: The screenshots show a logical progression of the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is a clear sequence of states: starting with just the sphere, linking the rig, overriding it, posing it, keyframing, and finally saving. The final result does not appear instantly in the first few frames.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply type the expected output paths or success messages into a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interactions are strictly within the Blender application interface and standard file browser dialogs.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The master rig is located at '/home/ga/BlenderProjects/assets/master_rig.blend' and contains the 'RoboArm_Collection'. The armature consists of Root, UpperArm, and Forearm bones. The final file must be saved as '/home/ga/BlenderProjects/shot_01_animated.blend'. A valid pose requires a rotation difference of at least 0.1 radians from the rest pose.
